name: fixture-05-cmo-consumer-brand
description: VP Marketing at a DTC beauty brand applying for CMO at a legacy CPG food brand. Strong digital + brand chops; stretch is traditional retail channel (grocery, mass) and a Fortune 500 matrix.

resume_data.name: Nora Whitfield
key_achievements: Grew annual revenue from $48M to $210M over 4 years at a DTC beauty brand through brand repositioning + retention-led LTV expansion.
Rebuilt the creative + performance marketing funnel in-house — cut agency spend 62% while improving blended CAC 31%.
Led the Sephora launch (exclusive 6-month window) that added $27M incremental revenue and shifted brand from 100% DTC to omnichannel.

requirements: Proven success building iconic consumer brands across retail and digital channels
Experience managing a marketing P&L in the $80M+ range
Demonstrated track record partnering with national retailers on category strategy
Fluency with CPG trade promotion, broker management, and shelf economics
Comfort leading a 60+ person matrixed marketing function
culture_cues: numbers-heavy — boardroom expects granular category data
collaborative across sales, R&D, and operations

letter_plan.opening_hook: Lead with the $48M → $210M growth story — modern brand building at scale — to establish credibility before the traditional-CPG stretch. Anchor on brand equity respect to signal cultural alignment with heritage.
body_points: 4x revenue growth + brand repositioning is the iconic-brand-building proof the JD demands; explicitly name the Sephora launch as the retail-partnership pattern that extends naturally to a national grocery partnership (category strategy, shelf placement, joint business planning).

In-house funnel rebuild + agency spend cut proves marketing-P&L discipline the Harvest board will want to see; frame the 62% agency reduction as the kind of matrix-team operating muscle (managing creative, analytics, media, CRM under one roof) that scales to the 60-person function.

Address the trade-promotion / broker / shelf-economics gap directly — DTC-first background means the field sales + trade marketing layer is the one domain to learn in the first 90 days; position the grocery-launch Sephora analog as proof that retail operating rhythm is not foreign.
letter_plan.closing_strategy: Acknowledge the heritage-brand equity explicitly — DTC brand builders sometimes underweight this, so being loud about respecting Harvest's 80-year category position is both honest positioning and cultural signal. Close on the omnichannel muscle as the future of the category.
